channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Community sets up memorial for eight children killed in Louisiana tragedy
item.description: Isang makeshift memorial ang itinayo ng mga residente sa labas ng isang bahay sa Louisiana kung saan walong bata ang nasawi sa isang malagim na insidente. Ayon sa ulat, nagtipon ang komunidad upang ma...

item.title: Concerns raised over attendees with China ties at Liberal fundraiser hosted by Mark Carney
item.description: A Liberal Party fundraiser attended by Prime Minister Mark Carney in the Toronto area has drawn scrutiny after reports that several attendees had connections to organizations aligned with the Chinese ...

item.title: Security Forces Kill Two Terrorists Including Ring Leader in Bannu Intelligence-Based Operation
item.description: According to the Inter-Services Public Relations (ISPR), security forces conducted an intelligence-based operation (IBO) in the Bannu district of Khyber Pakhtunkhwa on April 19, following reports of t...

item.title: Goodwill Edmonton Highlights Weekly Finds and Community Impact Through Thrift Shopping
item.description: This brief sponsored content piece highlights Goodwill’s ongoing efforts in Edmonton, focusing on both its retail offerings and its social mission. The article emphasizes that Goodwill thrift stores r...

item.title: Five factors keeping Arsenal in the Premier League title race despite defeat to Manchester City
item.description: Arsenal’s recent 2-1 defeat to Manchester City has dealt a significant blow to their Premier League title hopes, yet belief remains within the squad that the race is far from finished. Declan Rice exp...

item.title: How Tech CEOs Are Using AI to Centralize Control and Manage More Employees
item.description: 'Tech CEOs Think AI Will Let Them Be Everywhere at Once' explores how leading tech executives are turning artificial intelligence into tools for centralized management and omnipresent oversight. The a...

item.title: HiPP Recalls Baby Food in Central Europe After Suspected Tampering With Rat Poison
item.description: Several Central European countries have removed HiPP baby food products from store shelves after authorities discovered traces of rat poison in certain jars. The issue appears to be the result of deli...

item.title: Coalition weighs retaining housing tax changes to fund future tax cuts amid internal debate
item.description: Australia’s federal opposition is considering whether to retain potential changes to negative gearing and capital gains tax (CGT), despite historically opposing them, as a way to fund future election ...

item.title: Iran yet to confirm participation in next round of U.S. talks proposed in Pakistan
item.description: Iran has stated that it has not yet taken a final decision regarding its participation in the proposed second round of talks with the United States, which are expected to be held in Islamabad, Pakista...

item.title: Nancy Sinatra calls Trump’s use of Frank Sinatra’s ‘My Way’ an act of sacrilege
item.description: Nancy Sinatra has publicly condemned U.S. President Donald Trump for sharing a Truth Social post that features her father, Frank Sinatra, performing his classic song ‘My Way.’ Known for her long‑stand...

item.title: Rappler App Aims to Improve News Consumption and Community Engagement
item.description: The article introduces the Rappler app as a response to the growing problem of “doomscrolling” and information overload in today’s digital environment. It describes how many users typically switch bet...

item.title: High Court Reaffirms Compliance with Court Rules Amid Growing Use of AI in Legal Filings
item.description: The High Court in Kenya has issued a strong caution regarding the use of artificial intelligence in the preparation of legal documents, emphasising that technological tools cannot replace compliance w...

item.title: Key Developments Across Africa: From Deportations to Governance Updates
item.description: This week's African news brings a mix of developments spanning multiple nations. In Nigeria, discussions continue around petroleum policy and governance, while Kenya addresses ongoing concerns surroun...

item.title: Pakistan tightens security and traffic controls ahead of second round of US-Iran talks in Islamabad
item.description: Interior Minister Mohsin Naqvi held a meeting with US Charge d’Affaires Natalie Baker to finalise security arrangements ahead of the expected second round of US-Iran talks in Islamabad. The discussion...

item.title: Tanzania to Host 74th Miss World Pageant in 2027, Boosting Global Tourism and Investment Visibility
item.description: Tanzania has officially secured the rights to host the 74th Miss World pageant in 2027 after signing a landmark agreement that is expected to significantly elevate the country’s global profile. The an...

item.title: Student Loan Repayment Error: Government Offers Reprieve to Thousands
item.description: Thousands of university students in England facing demands to repay maintenance loans and childcare grants incorrectly awarded have been given a temporary reprieve by the government. Approximately 22,...

item.title: Don Jazzy Talks About Challenges Promoting Music with Gen Z Artists Like Ayra Starr
item.description: Popular Nigerian music executive and Mavin Records boss, Don Jazzy, don open up about one major challenge wey him dey face with some of him artistes, especially the younger Gen Z ones like Ayra Starr....

item.title: Judiciary intensifies training to safeguard sexual violence survivors during court proceedings
item.description: The Judiciary of Tanzania, through the Institute of Judicial Administration Lushoto (IJA) in partnership with Irish Rule of Law International (IRLI), has stepped up efforts to improve the handling of ...

item.title: How elite competition inside the ANC shapes political violence in South Africa
item.description: This article argues that South Africa’s protest waves are not explained by poverty and poor services alone, but also by power struggles inside the African National Congress (ANC). The authors say that...

item.title: Gauteng Health warns of rising malaria cases and urges public caution
item.description: The Gauteng Department of Health has issued a strong warning to residents to stay alert following a notable increase in malaria cases and related deaths in the province. Health authorities have urged ...
